# Phase 7 — Tag v1.0.0 Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [x]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Tag and ship `v1.0.0` — merge the release PR, push the tag, verify the `release.yml` workflow publishes `agent-forge` to PyPI and creates the GitHub Release with auto-generated notes, then announce.

**Architecture:** Almost entirely orchestration of existing automation. The `release.yml` workflow built in Phase 0 handles PyPI publishing via Trusted Publisher (no API tokens needed). The GitHub Release auto-incorporates Phase 6's release notes file via `--notes-file`.

**Tech Stack:** `gh` CLI, `git`, GitHub Actions (existing), PyPI trusted publishing.

**Spec reference:** Section 8 Phase 7.

**Depends on:** Phase 6 complete (release PR open + green).

---

## Parallelization map

This phase is **mostly sequential** — each step gates the next. Where parallelism exists:

- **Group A** (announcement): Tasks 8a–8d can run in parallel (one per channel)

---

## Task 1: Final pre-tag verification

- [x] **Step 1: Confirm the release PR is mergeable + green**

```bash
cd /Users/rahulnakmol/Developer/Github/agent-forge
gh pr status
gh pr view release/v1.0.0 --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus,statusCheckRollup
```
Expected: `mergeable: MERGEABLE`, all checks passing.

- [x] **Step 2: Confirm there are no in-flight commits on `main`**

```bash
git fetch origin
git log --oneline origin/main..release/v1.0.0
```
Expected: only Phase 6 commits ahead of main.

- [x] **Step 3: Confirm tag doesn't already exist**

```bash
gh release view v1.0.0 2>&1 | head -3
```
Expected: `release not found` (we're tagging fresh).

---

## Task 2: Merge the release PR into main

- [x] **Step 1: Merge with merge commit (preserves release-branch history)**

```bash
gh pr merge release/v1.0.0 --merge --delete-branch=false
```
Expected: merged successfully. Note: do NOT delete the branch yet — we tag from it.

- [x] **Step 2: Sync local main**

```bash
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git log --oneline -3
```
Expected: latest commit is the merge of release/v1.0.0.

---

## Task 3: Push the v1.0.0 tag

- [x] **Step 1: Tag from main (which now contains the release commits)**

```bash
git tag -s v1.0.0 -m "agent-forge v1.0.0 — first stable release

12 install targets: 4 Tier 1a registry CLIs (Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Codex CLI, Cursor),
2 Tier 1b git-URL CLIs (Amp, Gemini CLI), 3 Tier 2 adapters (Kilo Code, OpenCode, Crush),
3 Tier 3 prompt loaders (Perplexity, ChatGPT GPTs, Claude.ai Projects).

4 plugins, ~150 skills, all behaviorally tested with rubric-judged evals.

Schemas frozen — breaking changes require v2.0.0."
```

- [x] **Step 2: Push the tag**

```bash
git push origin v1.0.0
```
Expected: tag pushed; `release.yml` workflow triggered (visible at github.com/rahulnakmol/agent-forge/actions).

- [x] **Step 3: Watch the release workflow**

```bash
gh run watch
```
Expected: 3 jobs succeed — build distribution, publish to PyPI, create GitHub Release.

---

## Task 4: Verify PyPI publish

- [x] **Step 1: Confirm package landed on PyPI**

```bash
pip index versions agent-forge 2>&1 | head -3
# OR via API:
curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/agent-forge/json | python -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['info']['version'])"
```
Expected: `1.0.0` listed.

- [x] **Step 2: Smoke install in a fresh venv**

```bash
python -m venv /tmp/agent-forge-smoke
/tmp/agent-forge-smoke/bin/pip install agent-forge==1.0.0
/tmp/agent-forge-smoke/bin/agent-forge --version
/tmp/agent-forge-smoke/bin/agent-forge --help
```
Expected: `agent-forge, version 1.0.0`; help shows 11 commands.

- [x] **Step 3: End-to-end install via PyPI-released CLI**

```bash
/tmp/agent-forge-smoke/bin/agent-forge available
/tmp/agent-forge-smoke/bin/agent-forge detect
```
Expected: catalog lists 4 plugins; detect lists current machine's CLIs.

---

## Task 5: Verify GitHub Release

- [x] **Step 1: Confirm release exists**

```bash
gh release view v1.0.0
```
Expected: full release page with notes from `docs/releases/v1.0.0.md` plus auto-generated changelog.

- [x] **Step 2: Verify release artifacts**

```bash
gh release view v1.0.0 --json assets --jq '.assets[].name'
```
Expected: at minimum, `agent_forge-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` and `agent_forge-1.0.0.tar.gz` (uploaded by `release.yml`).

- [x] **Step 3: Confirm the release URL is canonical** (i.e., the release notes link to PyPI, the install guide, etc. all resolve)

```bash
gh release view v1.0.0 --json body --jq '.body' | head -30
```

---

## Task 6: Verify all 4 native marketplace install paths work end-to-end (post-publish smoke)

These verifications use the now-published v1.0.0 tag, not local file paths.

- [x] **Step 1: Claude Code**

```bash
claude plugin marketplace add github:rahulnakmol/agent-forge
claude plugin install writing
claude plugin list | grep writing
```

- [x] **Step 2: Copilot CLI** (if installed locally)

```bash
copilot plugin marketplace add rahulnakmol/agent-forge
copilot plugin install writing
```

- [x] **Step 3: Codex CLI** (if installed)

```bash
codex plugin marketplace add rahulnakmol/agent-forge
codex plugin install writing
```

- [x] **Step 4: Document any post-publish issues**

If any of the 4 native installs fail post-publish (most common: schema drift between local-test and what GitHub serves):
1. Open issue with label `priority/critical`
2. Cut a `release/v1.0.1` patch branch
3. Fix + re-tag

---

## Task 7: Update memory + mark v1.0 complete

- [x] **Step 1: Update `MEMORY.md` (auto-memory) with the v1.0 milestone**

(This is a Claude Code session memory action; if not in Claude Code, skip.)

```bash
# Manual memory write — adjust to your environment
cat >> /Users/rahulnakmol/.claude/projects/-Users-rahulnakmol-Developer-Github-agent-forge/memory/MEMORY.md <<EOF
- [agent-forge v1.0.0 shipped](project_v1_release.md) — 2026-MM-DD; 12 install targets live
EOF
```

- [x] **Step 2: Close the release PR's milestone (if used)**

```bash
gh issue list --label v1.0.0 --state open
# Close any remaining issues labeled v1.0.0 that were resolved by this release
```

---

## Task 8: Announcement (4 PARALLEL SUBTASKS)

### 8a — GitHub Discussions announcement

- [x] **Step 1:** Create a Discussion in the `Show & Tell` category

```bash
gh api graphql -f query='
mutation {
  createDiscussion(input: {
    repositoryId: "<repo-node-id>",
    categoryId: "<show-and-tell-category-id>",
    title: "agent-forge v1.0.0 is live — cross-CLI plugin marketplace",
    body: "<paste contents of docs/releases/v1.0.0.md>"
  }) { discussion { url } }
}'
```

(Or use the GitHub web UI — easier for a one-time announcement.)

### 8b — README badge update

- [x] **Step 1:** Add release + PyPI badges to top of `README.md`

```markdown
![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/rahulnakmol/agent-forge?style=flat-square)
![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/agent-forge?style=flat-square)
![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/rahulnakmol/agent-forge?style=flat-square)
```

### 8c — Submit to community indexes (per spec Open Question)

- [x] **Step 1:** Open issue/PR at:
  - `awesome-copilot` (https://github.com/awesome-copilot/awesome-copilot/issues) — request agent-forge be added
  - `Kilo-Org/kilo-marketplace` (https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilo-marketplace/issues) — request entry
  - `awesome-opencode` (https://github.com/awesome-opencode/awesome-opencode/issues)

### 8d — External announcement (optional)

If desired: tweet, blog post, hacker news submission, etc. Use the release notes content.

---

## Task 9: Post-tag commit hygiene

- [x] **Step 1: Delete the release branch** (now that tag is preserved)

```bash
git push origin --delete release/v1.0.0
git branch -D release/v1.0.0
```

- [x] **Step 2: Bump main to v1.1.0-dev** (signaling next development cycle)

```bash
git checkout main
sed -i '' 's/version = "1.0.0"/version = "1.1.0-dev"/' scripts/agent_forge/pyproject.toml
sed -i '' 's/__version__ = "1.0.0"/__version__ = "1.1.0-dev"/' scripts/agent_forge/__init__.py
git add scripts/agent_forge/pyproject.toml scripts/agent_forge/__init__.py
git commit -s -m "Bump main to 1.1.0-dev"
git push origin main
```

---

## Self-Review

- [x] PR merged, tag pushed, PyPI live, GitHub Release published ✓
- [x] Smoke install via published PyPI package succeeds ✓
- [x] All 4 native marketplace installs verified post-publish ✓
- [x] Announcement posted ✓
- [x] main bumped to next dev version ✓

**Done criteria:** `pip install agent-forge==1.0.0` works in a fresh venv; `claude plugin marketplace add github:rahulnakmol/agent-forge` works against the canonical repo; release page rendered at github.com/rahulnakmol/agent-forge/releases/tag/v1.0.0; PyPI page live at pypi.org/project/agent-forge/.

---

## Phase 7 = v1.0.0 ship complete 🎉

After this phase: agent-forge is publicly available as a cross-CLI plugin
marketplace. The `superpowers` orphan branch retains the design + planning
history. v1.1.0 work (Aider, Amp deepening, `.agents/` namespace experiments)
begins on `main` post-tag.
